summary: CC-0111 promotes the keystone GatewaySpec/GatewayParentRefSpec into internal/common/types (commonv1) as the single source of truth (Option B follow-up to #402). keystonev1alpha1 now type-aliases the commonv1 types so existing importers compile unchanged; the c5c3 api package deletes its curated copy and points ServiceKeystoneSpec.Gateway at *commonv1.GatewaySpec; the c5c3 L2 mapping in reconcile_keystone.go collapses to a single nil-safe DeepCopy with a DECISION comment. deepcopy and CRDs (bases + helm crds) were regenerated for all three modules; both CRD reference docs now describe GatewaySpec as a shared commonv1 type and cite CC-0111. Verified directly (all three modules build; full unit suites green; gofmt/gofumpt@v0.9.2/go vet clean) and independently confirmed by a 4-dimension multi-agent review (security/deps, quality/architecture, testing, CRD/docs) — all four returned APPROVED with no blocking or major findings.

tests_checklist.item: commonv1 GatewaySpec/GatewayParentRefSpec DeepCopy unit tests exist and pass (Annotations map + nested ParentRef independence, nil -> nil)
Alias transparency: pre-existing keystone gateway tests pass UNMODIFIED (no keystone *_test.go in the diff)
Repointed c5c3 gateway tests pass with identical assertions (only the type swapped c5c3v1alpha1 -> commonv1)
Edge cases covered (nil source clears gateway; map/pointer independence)
Full unit suites green for internal/common, operators/keystone, operators/c5c3 (go test -count=1)

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows the established commonv1 shared-type discipline (alongside DatabaseSpec/CacheSpec/ImageSpec/PolicySpec/SecretRefSpec)
Single source of truth: exactly one 'type GatewaySpec struct' + one 'type GatewayParentRefSpec struct', both in internal/common/types
No dead code: old struct defs and standalone deepcopy funcs fully removed from both operators
No orphaned imports introduced (keystonev1alpha1/commonv1/c5c3v1alpha1 still used where present)
gofmt, gofumpt (repo-pinned v0.9.2), and go vet clean on all changed Go files

security_checklist.item: Validation markers preserved (+kubebuilder:validation:MinLength=1 on Hostname and ParentRef.Name) -> minLength:1 in all four CRDs
No hardcoded secrets; no input-validation regression (required arrays + minLength byte-stable)
L1-no-keystone-dependency invariant intact: c5c3 api package imports only commonv1/apimachinery/controller-runtime (go list -deps shows no keystone import at L1)
check-spdx-reuse clean: no new uncovered file (types appended to existing types.go); regenerated zz_generated files keep SPDX headers

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is as simple as possible: L2 mapping reduced to one DeepCopy assignment
No go.mod/go.sum require or version changes across the three modules (check-go-workspace-deps stays aligned)
Gateway-block CRD validation schema byte-stable in both operators; only nested descriptions converge
bases/ and helm crds/ stay in lockstep for both operators; make verify-crd-sync green
Pure type-consolidation refactor with no external-exposure behaviour change (#402 semantics preserved)

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No duplicated code: the documented Option A curated copy in the c5c3 api package is removed
No speculative abstraction: alias kept only for backward compatibility of existing importers

fail_fast_checklist.item: DeepCopy is nil-safe (generated DeepCopy returns nil for a nil receiver), so a nil source deterministically clears the projected gateway

defensive_checklist.item: DeepCopy (over a direct pointer share) prevents aliasing between the ControlPlane spec and the projected Keystone spec; covered by an explicit independence unit test

issues_found.description: The L2 mapping was collapsed to DeepCopy() specifically to keep the projected Keystone gateway an independent object (per the new DECISION comment), but TestReconcileKeystone_GatewayProjection only asserts field equality of the projected gateway. A naive direct pointer share (keystone.Spec.Gateway = cp.Spec.Services.Keystone.Gateway) would still pass every assertion, so the independence guarantee is not exercised at the controller layer. The DeepCopy independence contract is, however, proven directly by TestGatewaySpec_DeepCopy in internal/common/types/types_test.go, so this is a suggestion, not a gap.
issues_found.evidence: reconcile_keystone.go: keystone.Spec.Gateway = cp.Spec.Services.Keystone.Gateway.DeepCopy(); test asserts only k.Spec.Gateway.ParentRef.Name == "openstack-gw" ... Annotations HaveKeyWithValue(...), with no post-reconcile mutation-of-source / unchanged-projection check.
issues_found.fix: Optional: after reconcile, mutate cp.Spec.Services.Keystone.Gateway.Annotations / ParentRef.Name and assert the already-projected k.Spec.Gateway is unaffected, locking the DeepCopy independence at the controller layer.

issues_found.description: The diff adds a second '### GatewaySpec' heading under the 'Shared Types (from internal/common/types)' section of keystone-crd.md, in addition to the canonical '## GatewaySpec' heading. Both slugify to the same anchor base #gatewayspec; VitePress de-duplicates, so the second becomes #gatewayspec-1. All cross-links (c5c3 controlplane-crd.md and the keystone self-links) target the bare #gatewayspec/#gatewayparentrefspec and still resolve to the canonical occurrences, so no link breaks — this is a cosmetic duplicate-heading smell only.
issues_found.evidence: grep '^#\+ GatewaySpec' keystone-crd.md returns both the canonical '## GatewaySpec' (~line 704) and the newly added '### GatewaySpec' (~line 1113); git show main:... returns only the canonical one, confirming line 1113 is new.
issues_found.fix: Optional: drop the duplicate '### GatewaySpec' heading and keep just the pointer prose, or rename it (e.g. '### GatewaySpec (shared)') so the Shared-Types section does not create a second heading slugging to #gatewayspec.

suggested_improvements: Optionally add a controller-layer independence assertion in TestReconcileKeystone_GatewayProjection (mutate the source gateway after reconcile, assert the projected Keystone gateway is unchanged) to lock the DeepCopy-over-pointer-share decision where it is made.
Optionally de-duplicate the second '### GatewaySpec' heading in keystone-crd.md so the Shared-Types listing points back to the canonical section without creating a #gatewayspec-1 anchor.

Out of scope but noted: architecture/docs/09-implementation/03-crd-implementation.md still shows GatewaySpec/GatewayParentRefSpec as local Keystone types in a Go code block. That sub-tree is untouched by this feature (and read-only per worktree rules); a future architecture-docs sync could align it with the commonv1 promotion.
next_steps: Approve and merge — all blocking and major criteria pass with concrete evidence; the two MINOR items are non-blocking suggestions.
If desired, apply the two AUTO_FIX MINOR suggestions (controller-layer independence assertion + duplicate-heading cleanup) in a quick follow-up commit.

detected_patterns.name: Shared CRD value types live once in commonv1; relocations leave a backward-compat alias
detected_patterns.applies_when: A CRD value type (a sub-spec used by more than one operator's CRD) needs to be shared between the keystone and c5c3 operators, or an existing per-operator copy is being consolidated
detected_patterns.description: Value types reused across operator CRDs are defined exactly once in internal/common/types and referenced as commonv1.<Type> from each operator's api package, rather than re-curated field-for-field per operator (which silently drifts). When an exported type is relocated out of an operator's api package into commonv1, a backward-compatible type alias (type X = commonv1.X) is left behind in the original package so existing in-repo and external importers compile unchanged. deepcopy is generated in commonv1 and the standalone per-operator deepcopy funcs are removed; the consuming DeepCopyInto references types.<Type>. This is the same discipline already applied to DatabaseSpec/CacheSpec/ImageSpec/PolicySpec/SecretRefSpec and now GatewaySpec.
